message:
feat(extension_scoring): add Value-of-Information planner for budget-aware extension selection

Adds a VoI (Value of Information) planning subsystem that selects which
extensions to evaluate based on their utility-to-overhead ratio within a
configurable overhead budget:

- VoiPlannerConfig: toggle, overhead budget (ms), max candidates,
  staleness window (minutes), and minimum utility score floor
- VoiCandidate: input type carrying utility score, estimated overhead,
  last-seen timestamp, and enabled flag
- plan_voi_candidates(): greedy knapsack by utility_per_ms ratio —
  sorts candidates by bang-for-buck, fills the overhead budget, and
  emits structured skip reasons (Disabled, MissingTelemetry,
  StaleEvidence, BudgetExceeded, BelowUtilityFloor) for audit
- VoiPlan: output with selected/skipped partitions, used and remaining
  overhead budgets for downstream telemetry

This enables the extension dispatcher to skip low-value extensions
during budget-constrained dispatch cycles rather than running all
extensions unconditionally.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
